questions.q: A steel rod of length 1.2 m and area of cross-section 2 x 10^-4 m^2 is subjected to a tensile force of 6000 N. Calculate the stress developed in the rod. Also, if the Young's modulus of steel is 2 x 10^11 Pa, calculate the strain developed in the rod.
questions.answer: To calculate the stress developed in the rod, we use the formula: stress = F/A, where F is the force applied and A is the area of cross-section. Substituting the given values, we get: stress = 6000 N / (2 x 10^-4 m^2) = 3 x 10^7 Pa. Next, to calculate the strain developed in the rod, we use the formula: strain = stress / Y, where Y is the Young's modulus of steel. Substituting the given values, we get: strain = (3 x 10^7 Pa) / (2 x 10^11 Pa) = 1.5 x 10^-4. Therefore, the stress developed in the rod is 3 x 10^7 Pa and the strain developed in the rod is 1.5 x 10^-4.
marking_scheme: Calculate the stress developed in the rod using the formula: stress = F/A (1 mark)
Calculate the strain developed in the rod using the formula: strain = stress / Y (1 mark)
State the numerical values of stress and strain (3 marks)

questions.q: A solid cylinder of radius 10 cm and height 20 cm is subjected to a compressive load of 2000 N. If the bulk modulus of the material is 1.5 x 10^11 Pa, calculate the fractional change in volume of the cylinder.
questions.answer: To calculate the fractional change in volume of the cylinder, we use the formula: ΔV/V = -P/B, where P is the pressure applied and B is the bulk modulus of the material. First, we need to calculate the pressure applied: P = F/A, where F is the force applied and A is the area of cross-section. The area of cross-section of the cylinder can be calculated using the formula: A = πr^2, where r is the radius of the cylinder. Substituting the given values, we get: A = π(0.1 m)^2 = 0.01π m^2. The pressure applied is then: P = 2000 N / (0.01π m^2) = 6.37 x 10^3 Pa. Next, we substitute the values of P and B into the formula: ΔV/V = - (6.37 x 10^3 Pa) / (1.5 x 10^11 Pa) = -4.23 x 10^-8.
marking_scheme: Calculate the pressure applied using the formula: P = F/A (1 mark)
Calculate the fractional change in volume using the formula: ΔV/V = -P/B (1 mark)
State the numerical value of the fractional change in volume (3 marks)

questions.q: A steel rod of length 1.5 m and area of cross-section 3 x 10^-4 m^2 is stretched by 0.1 m. Calculate the energy stored in the rod due to the stretching. If the Young's modulus of steel is 2 x 10^11 Pa, calculate the stress developed in the rod.
questions.topic: Energy Stored in a Stretched Rod
questions.answer: To calculate the energy stored in the rod, we use the formula: U = (1/2)FΔx, where F is the force applied and Δx is the displacement. First, we need to calculate the force applied: F = kΔx, where k is the spring constant. The spring constant can be calculated using the formula: k = YA/L, where Y is the Young's modulus of steel, A is the area of cross-section, and L is the length of the rod. Substituting the given values, we get: k = (2 x 10^11 Pa) (3 x 10^-4 m^2) / (1.5 m) = 4 x 10^6 N/m. The force applied is then: F = (4 x 10^6 N/m) (0.1 m) = 4 x 10^5 N. The energy stored in the rod is then: U = (1/2) (4 x 10^5 N) (0.1 m) = 2 x 10^4 J. Next, to calculate the stress developed in the rod, we use the formula: stress = F/A, where F is the force applied and A is the area of cross-section. Substituting the given values, we get: stress = (4 x 10^5 N) / (3 x 10^-4 m^2) = 1.33 x 10^9 Pa.
marking_scheme: Calculate the spring constant using the formula: k = YA/L (1 mark)
Calculate the force applied using the formula: F = kΔx (1 mark)
Calculate the energy stored in the rod using the formula: U = (1/2)FΔx (1 mark)
Calculate the stress developed in the rod using the formula: stress = F/A (1 mark)
State the numerical values of energy stored and stress (1 mark)

questions.q: A solid sphere of radius 15 cm and mass 2 kg is subjected to a compressive load of 500 N. If the bulk modulus of the material is 1.2 x 10^11 Pa, calculate the fractional change in volume of the sphere.
questions.answer: To calculate the fractional change in volume of the sphere, we use the formula: ΔV/V = -P/B, where P is the pressure applied and B is the bulk modulus of the material. First, we need to calculate the pressure applied: P = F/A, where F is the force applied and A is the area of cross-section. The area of cross-section of the sphere can be calculated using the formula: A = 4πr^2, where r is the radius of the sphere. Substituting the given values, we get: A = 4π(0.15 m)^2 = 0.0707π m^2. The pressure applied is then: P = 500 N / (0.0707π m^2) = 2.23 x 10^3 Pa. Next, we substitute the values of P and B into the formula: ΔV/V = - (2.23 x 10^3 Pa) / (1.2 x 10^11 Pa) = -1.86 x 10^-8.
marking_scheme: Calculate the pressure applied using the formula: P = F/A (1 mark)
Calculate the fractional change in volume using the formula: ΔV/V = -P/B (1 mark)
State the numerical value of the fractional change in volume (3 marks)

questions.q: A steel wire of length 2 m and area of cross-section 5 x 10^-5 m^2 is stretched by 0.05 m. Calculate the energy stored in the wire due to the stretching. If the Young's modulus of steel is 2.1 x 10^11 Pa, calculate the stress developed in the wire.
questions.topic: Energy Stored in a Stretched Wire
questions.answer: To calculate the energy stored in the wire, we use the formula: U = (1/2)FΔx, where F is the force applied and Δx is the displacement. First, we need to calculate the force applied: F = kΔx, where k is the spring constant. The spring constant can be calculated using the formula: k = YA/L, where Y is the Young's modulus of steel, A is the area of cross-section, and L is the length of the wire. Substituting the given values, we get: k = (2.1 x 10^11 Pa) (5 x 10^-5 m^2) / (2 m) = 5.25 x 10^5 N/m. The force applied is then: F = (5.25 x 10^5 N/m) (0.05 m) = 2.625 x 10^4 N. The energy stored in the wire is then: U = (1/2) (2.625 x 10^4 N) (0.05 m) = 6.5625 J. Next, to calculate the stress developed in the wire, we use the formula: stress = F/A, where F is the force applied and A is the area of cross-section. Substituting the given values, we get: stress = (2.625 x 10^4 N) / (5 x 10^-5 m^2) = 5.25 x 10^8 Pa.
marking_scheme: Calculate the spring constant using the formula: k = YA/L (1 mark)
Calculate the force applied using the formula: F = kΔx (1 mark)
Calculate the energy stored in the wire using the formula: U = (1/2)FΔx (1 mark)
Calculate the stress developed in the wire using the formula: stress = F/A (1 mark)
State the numerical values of energy stored and stress (1 mark)
